task: the fact that the rust code is quite untested does eek me out a bit. also feels like there is a lot of structure of the TS codebase missing - I am thinking that implementations of both the sdk-core and sdk packages in rust and subsequent reuse of these in all the FUSE code is still yet to be done.
user_feedback_text: the fact that the rust code is quite untested does eek me out a bit. also feels like there is a lot of structure of the TS codebase missing - I am thinking that implementations of both the sdk-core and sdk packages in rust and subsequent reuse of these in all the FUSE code is still yet to be done.

im kinda sorry to do this, but given that the staging database was nuked in phase 19.2 for the datastore migration, and the only user that has currently used the system is me, what say you we just remove all the migration code and make vault v2 the canonical source of truth everywhere dealing away with a whole bunch of migration code that will never be used.

task: yeah I think that we can document the changed scope in the phase context and have it marked as a gap, then gsd plan-phase --gaps to create a plan to close the gap, and then execute that phase. this will provide the cleanest way to document why the migration was removed mid-phase.
user_feedback_text: yeah I think that we can document the changed scope in the phase context and have it marked as a gap, then gsd plan-phase --gaps to create a plan to close the gap, and then execute that phase. this will provide the cleanest way to document why the migration was removed mid-phase.
